title: Add spoiler_text and sensitive fields to posts table and Status serialization
description:
## What
Add `spoiler_text TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''` column to posts table migration and include `spoiler_text` string field (never null, empty string when absent) and `sensitive: bool` field in Status serialization.

## Why
Clients expect these fields on every Status object; without them Ivory and Phanpy render incorrectly for posts with content warnings.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/statuses/:id and document exact JSON shape of spoiler_text and sensitive fields, including types and null vs empty-string behavior
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write the SQLite migration adding spoiler_text column, update the Status serializer to always emit spoiler_text (empty string, not null) and sensitive (bool)
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write an integration test that creates a post with a CW via the API, fetches it back, and asserts spoiler_text equals the input string and sensitive equals true

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/statuses/:id on a known CW post and inspect JSON — spoiler_text is a non-null string, sensitive is a bool
- Test approach: create post with spoiler_text set, fetch via GET /api/v1/statuses/:id, assert spoiler_text field present and correct, assert sensitive field is bool

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- spoiler_text is user content — HTML-escape on read (serialization), not on write (store raw)
- Migration must supply DEFAULT '' so existing rows are not null
- Status serializer must never emit null for spoiler_text
close_reason: spoiler_text already in schema and Status serialization
